practice.title: Count Even Numbers
practice.description:
Write a function `count_evens(nums)` that returns the count of even numbers in a list. What is the time complexity?

**Constraints:** `0 <= len(nums) <= 1000`, each number is an integer.
hints: Think about iterating through the list once — what is the Big-O of a single loop?
Use a counter variable. Increment it whenever `num % 2 == 0`.

challenge.title: Find the Duplicate
challenge.description:
Write a function `find_duplicate(nums)` that, given a list of integers where every element appears exactly once except one element which appears twice, returns the duplicate. Solve it in O(n) time.

**Constraints:** `2 <= len(nums) <= 10^4`, all values are positive integers.
hints: A naive O(n²) nested loop works but won't satisfy the constraint. Think about a data structure that gives O(1) lookup.
Use a set. Add each number; if it's already in the set, you've found the duplicate.
